findings.evidence: `WeilDivisor X` is `X →₀ ℤ` at `WeilDivisor.lean:44`; this PR already proves the general expansion at `FiniteSum.lean:71`, while the follow-on formulas are restricted to `m : X →₀ ℕ` at `FiniteSum.lean:98`, `:123`, `:132`, and `:142`; Mathlib has the general `Finsupp.sum_single` tool at `Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Finsupp/Basic.lean:420`.
findings.fix: State the general lemmas for every `D : WeilDivisor X`, e.g. `D = ∑ x ∈ D.support, coeff D x • ofPoint x`, `weightedDegree w D = ∑ x ∈ D.support, coeff D x * w x`, and `pushforward f D = ∑ x ∈ D.support, coeff D x • ofPoint (f x)`, then derive the `mapRange_natCast` corollaries from those plus `support_mapRange_natCast`.
findings.issue: `mapRange_natCast_eq_sum`, `degree_mapRange_natCast`, `weightedDegree_mapRange_natCast`, and `pushforward_mapRange_natCast` specialize ordinary formal-sum identities to natural multiplicities.

summary: The remaining natural-multiplicity `mapRange` algebraic API is still below the natural free-abelian-group level; the general divisor expansion is already present but the degree/weighted-degree/pushforward forms are only exposed for `X →₀ ℕ`.
